Harbour & Keel LLC PDPA/SGX Retention and Transfer Workflow Note re Verdant Atlas Limited (SGX-ST listed) – Project Banyan.
This note governs collection and use of PDPA personal data and MNPI in relation to Verdant Atlas Limited’s proposed acquisition of Lumen Terra Pte. Ltd., including vendor ESG/climate diligence and DPT (crypto) AML reviews.
Data classes: insider list entries (names, roles, timestamps), pre-announcement commercial terms (pricing collar proposals, earn-out bands), information-barrier logs, vulnerability scan outputs from cyber incident triage, and DPT wallet risk ratings.
For PDPA purposes, personal data includes data about an identifiable individual whether from the data or other information likely to be accessible (PDPA s.2), e.g., staff emails, deal room access logs, and wallet aliases linked to employees.
We maintain an information barrier between the Digital Assets Desk and ECM/M&A teams; wall-crossing requires approval by Ms. Asha Lim (DPO) and is recorded as IL: PRJ-BNYN-2O26 with e-sign at hxxps://intra.harbourkeel.sg/w f/PRJ-BNYN-2O26.
Trading blackout windows apply to all Verdant Atlas insiders from board mandate to SGX-ST announcement; no tipping or selective disclosure under the SFA and SGX Listing Rules is permitted.
Transfers: cross-border processing (e.g., review node “HarbourKeel KL-02”) is allowed only with comparable protection and written undertakings; data-at-rest uses AES-256 and “C old R oom” offline storage for draft SPA and green bond term sheets.
Retention: insider lists and wall-cross logs for 5 years post-announcement; cyber incident logs for 18 months unless an investigation requires longer; DPT wallet mapping for 24 months; ESG supplier audits retained until deal close + 2 years.
DSAR: individuals may request access/correction via dpo@harbourkeel.sg or +65 6999 4400; we may refuse, redact, or defer where disclosure would reveal MNPI or compromise a SGX clarification process, with reasons provided per PDPA.
Security controls include role-based access, dual-approval exports, and tamper-evident stamps; s ta f f IDs (e.g., EID: 7A-9 4 3) are pseudonymised in working copies, with key sheets held by the DPO.
For cyber incident containment involving Verdant Atlas pre-announcement ESG files, notify the PDPC within 3 days if significant harm or scale thresholds are met, and coordinate with the issuer on any SGX-ST announcement if required.
Educational only: our crypto literacy workshop reuses “wallet risk rating” vocabulary with synthetic datasets and does not include MNPI, deal-stage information, or advice; nothing herein constitutes or evidences a material adverse change.
Public sources note: carbon intensity baselines referenced from https://climate.example.sg/p u b l i c and vendor brochures; these public materials are not MNPI and may be shared in marketing without restriction.
Select third parties (e.g., Nimbus Cloud Pte. Ltd., Audit Forge LLP) receive limited data under PDPA-compliant transfer terms; acc ref: VA-D a t a-8893; crypto tag: wal: 0x9fFA...c3 (hashed) is stored separate from HR directories.
Insider list maintenance is overseen by Mr. Daryl Koh; ins ider l ist entries must reflect time of wall-cross and blackout status; breaches trigger internal reporting and potential MAS/SGX notifications.
operations: update blackout calendar, send clean-team pack to “Verdant Atlas – Clean Room” only, and archive “Project Banyan” chat exports by 23:00 SGT D+1; routine marketing content about green bonds remains non-sensitive and publicly sourced.
